verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 promises "give you a concrete plan" and immediately says "Turn on automated backups today"; B starts with "Confirm reality first" before the fix.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to the emergency sequence: "Turn on automated backups today" and "Take a manual snapshot right now"; B surveys stores and options, then asks to verify and choose.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent context: A says "production Aurora PostgreSQL" and "sqlite index.db / neo4j"; B adds "revenue/users/UTM", "PostHog", and "most likely the actual zero-backup store".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because the first two steps are immediate and plain; B has more commands but more branching and assumptions.
